answer:
Airtable uses token-based authentication for its API [1]. All requests must be made over HTTPS, and tokens are passed in the HTTP Authorization header using the Bearer scheme [1][2]. 

Personal access tokens are designed for personal integrations or private development and should not be shared [1][2]. OAuth access tokens are required for third-party integrations that allow other users to grant access to their data [1]. Legacy API key URL parameters are not supported [1].

Example of an authenticated request:

```
curl https://api.airtable.com/v0/YOUR_BASE_ID/YOUR_TABLE_ID_OR_NAME \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN"
```

You can manage personal access tokens at the Airtable developer portal [1][2]. OAuth flows are initiated by directing users to the Airtable authorization endpoint [3].

results.title: Getting started with Airtable's Web API | Airtable Support
